prompt: Implement a Python function `merge_intervals(intervals)` that takes a list of [start, end] integer pairs (in any order, possibly overlapping or touching) and returns a new list of non-overlapping intervals sorted by start, merging any that overlap OR merely touch (e.g. [1,3] and [3,5] merge to [1,5]). Do not mutate the input. Return ONLY a single ```python fenced code block containing the function definition and nothing else.

grading.tests:
assert merge_intervals([[1,3],[2,6],[8,10],[15,18]]) == [[1,6],[8,10],[15,18]]
assert merge_intervals([[1,4],[4,5]]) == [[1,5]]
assert merge_intervals([]) == []
assert merge_intervals([[5,6],[1,2],[3,4]]) == [[1,2],[3,4],[5,6]]
assert merge_intervals([[1,10],[2,3],[4,5]]) == [[1,10]]
_src = [[1,3],[2,6]]
assert merge_intervals(_src) == [[1,6]] and _src == [[1,3],[2,6]], 'must not mutate input'
